sections.paragraph:
with suff. חַחִי pl. חַחִים (with Dagesh forte implied, see Lehrg. § 38:1), properly a thorn, i.q. חֹוחַ which see. Hence-

(1) a ring, put through the perforated nostrils of animals which are to be tamed, and to which a cord was attached. 2Ki 19:28 Isa 37:29 Eze 29:4 (comp. Job 40:26 , and the remarks under the word חֹוחַ No. 2).

(2) a hook or clasp, to fasten together the garments of women (compare épingle, Germ. Spindel, from spinula, see Tac. Germ. 17), Exo 35:22. Others understand this to be a nose ring, elsewhere called נֶזֶם, see Bochart, Hieroz. i. p. 764. [Root חָנַח.]
